prompt:
We ship plugin bundles to edge boxes as loose directories today and rsync keeps half-applying them; I want a single-file container we control end to end. tar is overkill and I specifically want CRCs per entry so a flipped bit on those awful SD cards gets caught at read time, not at runtime. Format is already agreed with the firmware folks, so it's frozen:

  offset 0: magic, 4 bytes, ASCII "ARCP"
  offset 4: format version, 1 byte, currently 1
  offset 5: entry count, uint32 little-endian
  then the entry table, one record per entry, in stored order:
      name length, uint16 LE; name, UTF-8 bytes; payload size, uint64 LE; CRC-32 (IEEE) of the payload, uint32 LE
  then all payloads concatenated, same order as the table, no padding.

Implement package arc at the root of module go-arcpack, stdlib only:

  type File struct { Name string; Data []byte }
  type Entry struct { Name string; Size int64; CRC32 uint32 }
  func Write(path string, files []File) error
  func List(path string) ([]Entry, error)
  func ReadFile(path, name string) ([]byte, error)
  var ErrBadMagic, ErrVersion, ErrTruncated, ErrChecksum, ErrNotFound

Behavior I need:
- Write: entries stored in the order given. Duplicate names or an empty name: refuse with an error. Same input must produce byte-identical output every time — no timestamps, nothing environment-dependent. Empty archive (no files) is legal.
- List: reads ONLY the header and table — it must succeed even if payload bytes are damaged or the payload region is cut short, because the edge agent lists bundles before deciding what to pull.
- ReadFile: returns the named payload after verifying its CRC. Wrong magic -> ErrBadMagic; version we don't know -> ErrVersion; file ends before the data the header promised -> ErrTruncated; CRC disagrees -> ErrChecksum; name not in the table -> ErrNotFound. Damage to one entry must not poison reads of intact entries.

The suite in arc_test.go writes real files under a temp dir, corrupts them at specific offsets, and checks exact header bytes — that's the acceptance bar, don't modify it. Green under go test -race -timeout 30s ./...
